title: Explicit heat-equation update preserves constant states

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For any real diffusion number r and constant value u, the scalar explicit heat update of three equal neighboring values returns u.
assumptions: r and u are real numbers
the left, center, and right states are all u
informal.expected_conclusion: The scalar heat update preserves a constant state.

declarations.name: heatStep
declarations.statement: def heatStep (r uLeft u uRight : Real) : Real := heatLeftWeight r * uLeft + heatCenterWeight r * u + heatRightWeight r * uRight

declarations.name: heatStep_const
declarations.statement: theorem heatStep_const (r u : Real) : heatStep r u u u = u

tasks.input: Translate this explicit heat-equation finite-difference theorem into Lean 4: For any real diffusion number r and constant value u, the scalar explicit heat update of three equal neighboring values returns u.
tasks.target: theorem heatStep_const (r u : Real) : heatStep r u u u = u

tasks.input: Given the heat-equation scaffold definitions and theorem statement, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof by unfolding heatStep and the weight definitions, then using ring.
